identity.short_name: Prime-square half-order with LTE valuation and carry-coupled binomial valuation

problem_spec.summary: Find the least exponent n satisfying: (i) a difference divisibility at a prime, (ii) a sum divisibility at a prime-square, (iii) an exact p-adic valuation via LTE, and (iv) a fixed binomial p-adic valuation via digit carries; then output n modulo a stated modulus.
givens: $v_p(m)$ is the largest $e\ge 0$ such that $p^e\mid m$
ask: Determine the least $n\ge 1$ such that $23\mid(a^n-b^n)$, $13^2\mid(a^n+b^n)$, $v_5(a^n-b^n)=4$, and $v_5\!\left(\binom{2n}{n}\right)=1$. Let $N$ be this least $n$. Output the unique integer in $\{0,1,\dots,998\}$ congruent to $N$ modulo $999$.

parameter(s).name: $b$
parameter(s).purpose: base for $a^n\pm b^n$; must be invertible modulo all moduli used

parameter(s).name: $r$
parameter(s).domain: odd prime distinct from $q$ with $r\nmid ab$
parameter(s).purpose: sum divisibility constraint at prime-square modulus $r^2$ via lifted order and a forced half-order

parameter(s).name: $p$
parameter(s).domain: odd prime distinct from $q,r$ with $p\nmid ab$ and $p\mid(a-b)$
parameter(s).purpose: valuation prime for LTE-controlled exact depth

parameter(s).name: $c$
parameter(s).purpose: target binomial valuation $v_p\!\left(\binom{2n}{n}\right)=c$ via digit carries

invariant(s).name: difference_order_mod_q
invariant(s).statement: Let $g_q\equiv ab^{-1}\pmod q$ and $t=\operatorname{ord}_q(g_q)$. Then $q\mid(a^n-b^n)$ iff $t\mid n$.

invariant(s).name: prime_square_sum_to_half_order
invariant(s).statement: Let $g\equiv ab^{-1}\pmod{r^2}$ and $T=\operatorname{ord}_{r^2}(g)$. If $T$ is even then $g^{T/2}$ is the unique nontrivial solution to $x^2\equiv 1\pmod{r^2}$, hence $g^{T/2}\equiv -1\pmod{r^2}$ and $r^2\mid(a^n+b^n)$ iff $n\equiv T/2\pmod T$.

invariant(s).name: LTE_exact_valuation_difference
invariant(s).statement: If $p\mid(a-b)$ and $p\nmid ab$, then $v_p(a^n-b^n)=v_p(a-b)+v_p(n)$. Hence $v_p(a^n-b^n)=k$ forces $v_p(n)=k-v_p(a-b)$.

invariant(s).name: binomial_valuation_digit_carries
invariant(s).statement: $v_p\!\left(\binom{2n}{n}\right)$ equals the number of base-$p$ carries when adding $n+n$.

essential_assumption(s): $\gcd(a,b)=1$ and $q r p\nmid b$ so $b^{-1}$ exists modulo $q$ and $r^2$.
For the prime-square order lift, require $(ab^{-1})^d\not\equiv 1\pmod{r^2}$ so that the order multiplies by $r$.
uniqueness_certificate(s): The order constraints force $n$ into a single residue class modulo $\operatorname{lcm}(\operatorname{ord}_q(g_q),\operatorname{ord}_{r^2}(g))$. LTE fixes $v_p(n)$ (and excludes higher $p$-divisibility when required). The binomial valuation is a carry-count invariant, which selects the unique smallest admissible element of the resulting arithmetic progression; hence the least $n$ and its reduction modulo $M$ are unique.

technique(s).name: modular_obstructions
inputs: $a^n\pm b^n$ modulo primes and prime squares
technique(s).purpose: rewrite divisibility as congruences for $g\equiv ab^{-1}$

technique(s).name: square_root_uniqueness_mod_prime_power
technique(s).purpose: identify the unique nontrivial square root of $1$ as $-1$

technique(s).name: LTE_valuation
technique(s).purpose: translate exact valuation into an exact constraint on $v_p(n)$

technique(s).name: chinese_remainder_theorem
inputs: multiple congruence and divisibility constraints on $n$
technique(s).purpose: solve coupled congruences and parametrize solutions as an arithmetic progression

theorem(s).name: Legendre_formula
theorem(s).role: Gives $v_p(m!)$ and implies a digit-sum expression for $v_p\!\left(\binom{2n}{n}\right)$.

theorem(s).name: digit_sum_carry_identity
theorem(s).role: Relates digit sums to number of carries in base-$p$ addition.

closest_first_principle_concept_applied: Unit groups modulo prime powers and digit-carry invariants jointly control admissible exponents.

critical_trick(s): Use prime-square order lifting to turn $r^2\mid(a^n+b^n)$ into a forced half-order congruence for $n$, combine with $q\mid(a^n-b^n)$ by CRT, enforce an exact $p$-adic depth via LTE, then use digit carries to force $v_p\!\left(\binom{2n}{n}\right)=c$ and select the unique smallest progression element.

library(s).name: sympy
library(s).reason: compute modular inverses and multiplicative orders; fast modular exponentiation

task(s).name: verify_orders_and_minimum
task(s).reason: verify $\operatorname{ord}_{23}(g)=11$ and $\operatorname{ord}_{169}(g)=52$, check all four constraints at $N=50050$, and scan $1\le n\le 50050$ to confirm minimality using modular arithmetic and the digit-sum formula for $v_5\!\left(\binom{2n}{n}\right)$.
